title: Modularize MineflayerAdapter: split index.js into focused modules
description:
Break up the monolithic `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` (~1500 lines) into focused modules. Per the audit, one file owning connection/auth, logging, chat filtering, world scanning, movement, mining, placement, wandering, crafting, smelting, and event emission weakens locality and makes deletion tests impossible.

Proposed module split (all under `MineflayerAdapter/`):

```
MineflayerAdapter/
  index.js              → entry point: imports and wires modules
  lib/
    config.js           → all tunable constants, env vars, defaults
    logging.js          → logStructured(), LOG_DIR setup
    bot-factory.js      → createBot(), loadPlugin(), authentication
    ws-server.js        → WebSocket server, handshake, command queue, dispatch routing
    events/
      bot-events.js     → spawn, health, move, death, kicked, error, game
      chat-handler.js   → chat filter, system message classification, chat events
      inventory.js      → playerCollect, updateSlot, sendBotStatus
      pathfinder.js     → gotoWithTimeout, path_update/goal_reached/path_stop listeners
    actions/
      move.js           → move action handler
      mine.js           → mine action handler (findBestBlock, dig loop)
      place.js          → place action handler
      craft.js          → craft action handler
      smelt.js          → smelt action handler
      wander.js         → wander action handler
      findFlatArea.js   → flat area scanner
      findReachableBlock.js → reachable block query
      stop.js           → emergency stop handler
    world/
      telemetry.js      → sendRichStatus, sendLocalShape, entity scanning
```

Implementation strategy:
1. Start with the lowest-risk split: extract `config.js` and `logging.js` (pure functions, no bot dependency).
2. Then extract `ws-server.js` (WebSocket logic already self-contained).
3. Then extract each action handler into its own module.
4. Then extract event listeners.
5. Keep `index.js` as the import-and-wire entry point, NOT a dumping ground.
6. Each extracted module exports a function that receives the shared state it needs (bot, sendEvent, logStructured, tunable constants).

Deletion test for each module: "Can I delete this module and only affect that one feature?"

Acceptance:
- `index.js` is under 200 lines (wiring only).
- Each action lives in its own file.
- No circular imports.
- All existing behavior preserved.
- Existing tests pass.

Refs: `msa_planning_mineflayer_audit_20260621_1437.md` Finding 5 (adapter monolith); `mineflayer-adapter-research.md` architectural recommendations

comments.author: SteveBot
comments.body: Sprint 52 Wave 1: Extracted config.js (60 lines of tunable constants), logger.js (logStructured + fs setup), chatFilter.js (SYSTEM_MESSAGE_PATTERNS + bot.on('chat') handler), and stopState.js (emergency stop handling) from index.js (1529→1505 lines). Wired config.js and logger.js imports into index.js. chatFilter.js and stopState.js modules created but not yet wired (require bot instance DI pattern due to tight coupling with bot state). Next: extract action handlers into actions/ directory.
